title: How hard should I massage after filler？ My top massage tip
filename: How hard should I massage after filler？ My top massage tip.en.vtt
excerpt: So how hard do i need to massage after a dermal filler procedure [Music] so firstly this depends on what your goal is i like to do a process of i call it smooth it don't move it so you're just smoothing the area you're feeling for little collections of high pressures or high pres…

segments.text: so how hard do i need to massage after a dermal filler procedure [Music] so firstly this depends on what your goal is i like to do a process of i call it smooth it don't move it so you're just smoothing the area you're feeling for little collections of high pressures or high pressure zones where the filler may have naturally collected in the tissue and just smoothing them in so that there aren't any discrete lumps or bumps so that's smoothing it not moving it and it's a relatively gentle massage but the best tip i can give you is that it's a two-way process massaging when i've seen new injectors massaging it's almost like they're replicating the movement but they're not actually feeling what they're doing you've got to feel and squeeze at the same time um i liken it sometimes to if you've got a little lump or a bump for example in a lip or anywhere it's a bit like being blind you you're feeling for the example i use is a like a pee on a plate somewhere in the room and you start out gently feeling for where it is and there's not

a lot of pressure then you feel the fullness and then when you've got your finger just over the fullest part of that lump or in this case the pee you push on it until you feel it squelch and then it's gone so you're feeling at the same time as massaging and strategically squeezing little lumps or bumps that you might feel and smoothing over the rest so it's a two-way process feel and massage at the same time and only put the higher levels of pressure strategically when you know there's an imperfection in terms of the amount of filler in a particular area hope that helps you

transcript: so how hard do i need to massage after a dermal filler procedure [Music] so firstly this depends on what your goal is i like to do a process of i call it smooth it don't move it so you're just smoothing the area you're feeling for little collections of high pressures or high pressure zones where the filler may have naturally collected in the tissue and just smoothing them in so that there aren't any discrete lumps or bumps so that's smoothing it not moving it and it's a relatively gentle massage but the best tip i can give you is that it's a two-way process massaging when i've seen new injectors massaging it's almost like they're replicating the movement but they're not actually feeling what they're doing you've got to feel and squeeze at the same time um i liken it sometimes to if you've got a little lump or a bump for example in a lip or anywhere it's a bit like being blind you you're feeling for the example i use is a like a pee on a plate somewhere in the room and you start out gently feeling for where it is and there's not a lot of pressure then you feel the fullness and then when you've got your finger just over the fullest part of that lump or in this case the pee you push on it until you feel it squelch and then it's gone so you're feeling at the same time as massaging and strategically squeezing little lumps or bumps that you might feel and smoothing over the rest so it's a two-way process feel and massage at the same time and only put the higher levels of pressure strategically when you know there's an imperfection in terms of the amount of filler in a particular area hope that helps you
